wiki.summary: “Sienna” by The Marías reflects on lost plans, unfulfilled dreams, and the emotional aftermath of a breakup. The song is about a planned child that María Zardoya (the band’s vocalist) had envisioned with Josh Conway (the band’s drummer), which never came to be because their relationship ended. The song was written by the ex-couple. Throughout the lyrics, Sienna channels that sense of loss and longing into music, creating a way to “live” the potential and emotional connection that never materialized. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Sienna” by The Marías reflects on lost plans, unfulfilled dreams, and the emotional aftermath of a breakup. The song is about a planned child that María Zardoya (the band’s vocalist) had envisioned with Josh Conway (the band’s drummer), which never came to be because their relationship ended. The song was written by the ex-couple. Throughout the lyrics, Sienna channels that sense of loss and longing into music, creating a way to “live” the potential and emotional connection that never materialized. The song acts both as personal catharsis and a tribute to what could have been, showing how art allows Sienna to preserve and process a life that existed only in plans and imagination. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
